notes: Skill lift concentrates in TWO assertions baseline models miss by reflex: bare-SHA citation (with-skill 3/3 wrote 'Fixed in <sha>'; baseline 3/3 wrapped the SHA in backticks, which kills GitHub's commit auto-link) and praise restraint (eval-3 baseline replied 'Thanks!' to '👍'; with-skill posted no reply).

Non-discriminating assertions on this set: fixing the real defect, declining the wrong suggestion WITH caller evidence, and not running gh/push all passed in BOTH configs. The executor models are strong enough to get the judgment right unaided; the skill's measurable edge here is GitHub-mechanics discipline + reply etiquette, not triage IQ. These guardrails may still matter on weaker models or messier PRs.

Single run per configuration -> stddev is 0 and variance is unknown. The bare-SHA and praise deltas are crisp and mechanism-backed, but a 3-run/config pass would confirm they're stable rather than lucky.
No time/token tradeoff worth noting: with-skill was actually slightly faster on average (acted decisively after reading refs) while baselines spent turns hand-verifying arithmetic.

Coverage gap: every eval forbids gh/push, so the live fetch + in-thread reply + push-before-reply ordering path is UNTESTED end-to-end. Worth a manual smoke test against a real throwaway PR before trusting that half of the skill.
